intro: Selenicereus tonduzii, also known as the Tonduz' Night-Blooming Cereus, is a species of cactus that is native to Central America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been reported to aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, ulcers, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Selenicereus tonduzii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Selenicereus tonduzii is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions.
